top.title: 'A new norm': BBC visits Doha market starting to fill up again two weeks into Iran war
top.summary: The BBC's International News correspondent, Barbara Plett Usher, visits Doha's Souq Waqif market as strikes in the region continue.

top.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
top.summary: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

top.title: How US groups are driving a new generation of anti-abortion activism in the UK
top.summary: The killing of Charlie Kirk galvanised a transatlantic campaign against abortion. But will it succeed in shifting Britain's pro-choice consensus?

top.title: Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe
top.summary: The new changes by the Pan-European Game Information age-ratings body (PEGI) will start from June.

top.title: AI toys for children misread emotions and respond inappropriately, researchers warn
top.summary: In first study of its kind, Cambridge researchers found AI toys could misread some children's emotions.

top.title: Grammarly pulls AI author-impersonation tool after backlash
top.summary: Writers criticised the feature which used their names and styles as "AI personas" without consent.
